nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Elementary Latin I
nodes.description: For students who present less than two years of high school Latin.
nodes.other_requirements: For students who present less than two years of high school Latin. Open to Degree and PACE students; Co-requisite: LAT 1100 L01 or LAT 1100 L02

nodes.title: Topics in Roman Prose
nodes.description: Representative topics: Republican Prose (Caesar, Sallust, Cicero), Roman Oratory (Cicero's De Oratore, Orator, Brutus), Historians of the Empire (Augustus, Tacitus, Suetonius, Ammianus Marcellinus), Roman Letters (Cicero, Horace, Pliny). May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Credit not awarded for both LAT 4600 and LAT 5600. Prerequisite: LAT 3200.
nodes.other_requirements: instructor permission; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Survey of Latin Literature
nodes.description: Selections from principal Roman authors. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: LAT 2100, LAT 2200, or equivalent.

nodes.title: Elementary Latin II
nodes.description: For students who present less than two years of high school Latin. Prerequisite: LAT 1100 or equivalent.

nodes.title: Intermediate Latin I
nodes.description: Selections from Cicero and other prose authors. Prerequisite: LAT 1200 or equivalent.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Intermediate Latin II
nodes.description: Selections from Vergil and Ovid. Prerequisite: LAT 1200 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Topics in Roman Poetry
nodes.description: Representative topics: Roman Epic Poetry (Lucretius, Vergil, Ovid), Roman Lyric Poets (Catullus, Horace, Propertius, and Tibullus), Roman Comedy (Plautus and Terence), Satire (Horace, Persius, Juvenal, Petronius). May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Credit not awarded for both LAT 5650 and LAT 4650. Prerequisite: Classics Graduate student.
